summary: This cluster focuses on the cryopreservation of red algal species within the genus *Porphyra* from Korea, specifically *P. serata*, *P. yezoensis*, *P. tenera*, *P. pseudolinearis*, and *P. dentata*. The research established a two-step cooling method using liquid nitrogen and cryoprotectants such as dimethylsulfoxide and sorbitol to maintain sporothalli viability. Key findings demonstrated that this technique offers a viable long-term preservation alternative to liquid culture media, adapting principles from land plant freezing protocols to address the limitations of existing algal preservation methods.
